text:
Working with APIs is a common task for modern developers. An API allows different software applications to communicate with each other. Typically, you make HTTP requests to an API endpoint to get or send data. The data is often returned in JSON format, which is easy for both humans and machines to read.

Authentication is important when accessing many APIs. You may need an API key or a token to prove your identity. Also, be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make within a certain time.

Finally, always include error handling in your code. APIs might return errors due to invalid inputs or server problems, so checking for these issues makes your application more reliable.
